title: L4: Numbers in Binary
description: Using 4-bit place values to convert small denary numbers to binary and back.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now Activity

Quick check from Binary Digits.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr4-dna',questions:[{"prompt":"What is a bit?","answers":["binary digit","a binary digit"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in 1011?","answer":"4","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Is 1002 binary? Type yes or no.","answers":["no","n"],"right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: 4-bit Place Values
steps.content:
4-bit place values

In this lesson every binary number has four bits. Always place the bits under 8, 4, 2 and 1. A 1 means add the place value. A 0 means do not add it.

8421
1011

For 1011, add only the columns with a 1: 8 + 2 + 1 = 11.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr4-places-check',questions:[{"prompt":"In 4-bit binary, what are the place values from left to right?","answers":["8 4 2 1","8,4,2,1","8, 4, 2, 1"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"In 1011, which values are active? Type the total.","answer":"11","right":"Correct: 8 + 2 + 1 = 11."}]});

steps.title: Key Ideas: 8, 4, 2, 1
steps.content:
Key ideas

For this lesson, every binary number uses four columns: 8, 4, 2 and 1. Put one bit under each column.

8421
1010

To convert binary to denary: add only the column values that have a 1. To convert denary to binary: choose the columns that add to the denary number.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr4-vocab-check',questions:[{"prompt":"A 1 in a binary column means A = add it, B = ignore it. Type A or B.","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A 0 in a binary column means A = add it, B = ignore it. Type A or B.","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Binary to Denary
steps.content:
Step through binary to denary

Click through a small 4-bit conversion.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({completeOnEnd:false,containerId:'y8dr4-stepper-1',steps:[{"title":"We are converting 1011 to denary","html":"
1011
","note":"Line the bits up under 8, 4, 2 and 1."},{"title":"Find the active columns","html":BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,0,1,1],[8,4,2,1]),"note":"The 1s are under 8, 2 and 1. The 0 under 4 means ignore 4."},{"title":"Add the active columns","html":"

8 + 2 + 1 = 11

","note":"1011 in denary is 11."}]});BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr4-guided1-check',questions:[{"prompt":"Convert 1011 to denary.","answer":"11","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Which column is ignored in 1011? Type the place value.","answer":"4","right":"Correct: the 4 column has 0."}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Denary to Binary
steps.content:
Step through denary to binary

Click through how to build a 4-bit number.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({completeOnEnd:false,containerId:'y8dr4-stepper-2',steps:[{"title":"We are converting 13 to binary","html":"
13
","note":"Check the place values from left to right: 8, then 4, then 2, then 1."},{"title":"Choose columns that fit","html":"
Place valueDoes it fit?Left over
8Yes: 13 includes 85
4Yes: 5 includes 41
2No: 1 is less than 21
1Yes: 1 includes 10
","note":"Write 1 when a column fits, and 0 when it does not."},{"title":"Read off the binary number","html":BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,1,0,1],[8,4,2,1]),"note":"13 in 4-bit binary is 1101."}]});BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr4-guided2-check',questions:[{"prompt":"Convert 13 to 4-bit binary.","answer":"1101","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"When building 13, does the 2 column fit? Type yes or no.","answers":["no","n"],"right":"Correct: after 8 and 4, only 1 is left."}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Build Smaller Numbers
steps.content:
Step through smaller numbers

These examples show why leading zeros matter in 4-bit binary.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createStepper({completeOnEnd:false,containerId:'y8dr4-stepper-3',steps:[{"title":"Build 2","html":BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,0,1,0],[8,4,2,1]),"note":"Only the 2 column is used. The answer is 0010, not just 10, because we are using 4 bits."},{"title":"Build 5","html":BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,1,0,1],[8,4,2,1]),"note":"Use 4 and 1. The answer is 0101."},{"title":"Build 8","html":BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,0,0,0],[8,4,2,1]),"note":"Use only the 8 column. The answer is 1000."},{"title":"Build 15","html":BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,1,1,1],[8,4,2,1]),"note":"Use every column. 8 + 4 + 2 + 1 = 15."}]});BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr4-guided3-check',questions:[{"prompt":"Convert 2 to 4-bit binary.","answer":"0010","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 5 to 4-bit binary.","answer":"0101","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Mini Check: Active Columns
steps.content:
Mini check: active columns

Before converting, practise spotting the active columns. Type the total of the active columns.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr4-mini-check',questions:[{prompt:'Which total is active?',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,0,0,1],[8,4,2,1]),answer:'1',right:'Correct: only 1 is active.'},{prompt:'Which total is active?',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,1,0,0],[8,4,2,1]),answer:'4',right:'Correct: only 4 is active.'},{prompt:'Which total is active?',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,0,0,0],[8,4,2,1]),answer:'8',right:'Correct: only 8 is active.'},{prompt:'Which total is active?',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,1,1,0],[8,4,2,1]),answer:'6',right:'Correct: 4 + 2 = 6.'},{prompt:'Which total is active?',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,0,1,1],[8,4,2,1]),answer:'11',right:'Correct: 8 + 2 + 1 = 11.'}]});

steps.title: Checked Practice 1
steps.content:
Checked practice: binary to denary

Convert each 4-bit binary number to denary.

steps.title: Checked Practice 2
steps.content:
Checked practice: denary to binary

Convert each denary number to 4-bit binary.

steps.title: Extension: Mixed 4-bit Challenge
steps.content:
Extension challenge

Fast finishers can complete a mixed set. These stay within 4-bit numbers.

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless practice

Answer 5 generated 4-bit conversion questions correctly to complete this step.
steps.js: (function(){var bank=[{"prompt":"Convert 0011 to denary.","answer":"3","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 1010 to denary.","answer":"10","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 15 to 4-bit binary.","answer":"1111","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 8 to 4-bit binary.","answer":"1000","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 7 to 4-bit binary.","answer":"0111","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 0100 to denary.","answer":"4","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 2 to 4-bit binary.","answer":"0010","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 1101 to denary.","answer":"13","right":"Correct."}];var qs=[];for(var i=0;i<5;i++){qs.push(bank[Math.floor(Math.random()*bank.length)]);}BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr4-endless',questions:qs});})();

steps.title: End of Lesson Exam
steps.content:
End of lesson exam

Answer all questions.

quizQuestions.q: What are the 4-bit place values from left to right?
In 1101, which place value is not active?
What does a 0 in a binary column mean?
options: Do not include that place value
